sections.paragraph:
to cleave, specially to furrow the ground, Psa 141:7. (Arab. فلح id.; hence to till a field, فَلَّاحُ a husbandman. Compare Ch. and as to the origin under the root פָּלָא.

Piel-

(1) to cleave, pierce, e.g. as a dart the liver, Pro 7:23 Job 16:13 to cut up, e.g. gourds, 2Ki 4:39.

(2) to cause (young ones) to cleave the womb and break forth, i.e. to bring forth, Job 39:3. Compare פָּטַר.
